title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Remind user to check connection or use podman machine
body:
~~Change error message to be more helpful when trying to run a remote
command by reminding the user to start their podman machine vm.~~

Remind user to check their remote linux connection or use podman
machine. Move the warning from bindings to cmd/podman.


Fixes #11419

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
@baude PTAL

I share @Luap99's thoughts.  Maybe we can use typed errors and catch them in `cmd/podman/machine` to do the wrapping.

> @baude PTAL
> 
> I share @Luap99's thoughts. Maybe we can use typed errors and catch them in `cmd/podman/machine` to do the wrapping.

I also agree.  @ashley-cui lemme know if you need help or clarification on this.

Sure, I'll give this another stab. I think this wouldn't go in  `cmd/podman/machine` though, it would have to go somewhere in `/cmd/podman/root` or `cmd/podman/main`?  Since any remote podman command that fails should display this reminder

Update: this is definitely kinda hacky since we're just comparing error strings, but connection errors can happen from more than one place. If anyone has any better idea let me know, this is all that I could think of :)

If hacky helps out users, then it is good hacky.
LGTM
@baude @Luap99 While moving the connection code into c/common, I will expose new Err* to help clean up code that consumes the new package.  /cc @ashley-cui

Ok once new code is commmitted we can make changes, for now I am going to merge.
/lgtm
/approve
